bull: Top-line momentum — revenue grew +35.1% YoY in FY2026 Q1 to $1134.1B, with TTM revenue of $4103.9B confirming the run-rate. Growth at this pace is unusual for a company of this scale and reflects either share gains, pricing power, or new-product cycles depending on the line-item mix in the Fundamental tab.

Profitability premium — net margin of 50.5% in the latest quarter sits well above the cross-sector ~10–15% benchmark, indicative of either structural pricing power or operating leverage from scale. Expect this to compress only modestly through-cycle.
Margin trajectory — net margin expanded +7.3pp over the last 4 quarters in the dataset. Management's cost-out commentary on recent calls is showing up in the P&L, not just the press release.

Trend regime constructive — spot $401.62 sits +27.1% above the 200-DMA ($316.10). The long-term moving average has been a reliable reference for institutional capital and remains supportive of the directional bias.

Options market complacent — front-month implied move of just 4.2% suggests the market is not pricing in a binary outcome at the next print. Asymmetric setup if the company delivers a positive surprise — limited downside priced, full upside available.
bear: Valuation premium — 24.0x forward P/E vs peer median of 17.5x — a 37% premium. The setup requires continued out-execution to maintain; any reversion to peer-median multiples compresses the stock meaningfully even before any earnings revision.
52-week extension — at 92% of the 52-week range, the stock is one bad print away from a 10–15% reset back into the middle of the range. Position size accordingly.
trigger_up: Revenue growth re-accelerates above 38% YoY on next print — confirms the demand environment hasn't degraded into the back half.
Net margin expands above 52.0% — confirms pricing power and/or cost discipline thesis.
Management raises FY guide on next quarterly call — direct upward revision to consensus.
Clean breakout above $419.50 (52-week high) on volume confirmation — technical signal that institutional accumulation is sustained.
trigger_down: Revenue growth decelerates below 32% YoY — signals demand has materially softened versus the latest disclosed run-rate.
Net margin compresses below 48.5% — flags either input-cost pressure or competitive dynamics shifting unfavorably.
Management cuts FY guide on next quarterly call — direct downward revision; historically sees 5–15% drawdown on print.
Decisive break below the 52-week low at $191.76 — technical confirmation that the long-term trend has rolled over.
bottom_line:
BULLISH. Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company Limited (TSM) printed $1134.1B in revenue in FY2026 Q1 (+35.1% YoY), net margin of 50.5%. At 24.0x forward P/E, the stock carries a 37% premium to peers (17.5x median) — the multiple needs continued execution to defend.

Technical regime is constructive: spot $401.62 above the 200-DMA ($316.10, +27.1%) with neutral momentum (RSI 52). Sell-side consensus PT of $250 implies -37.8% upside. The options market is pricing a ±4.2% move by the front-month expiry — a complacent read on near-term risk. 30-day implied move is ±12.3%; 90-day is ±23.0%.
